$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `image_profiles` repo (Phase-2). One
committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-image-profiles-fixture.ts + image-profiles-tier2.ts)
and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness image_profiles tier-2 test).
What this repo banks: the Taggable lineage (user-scoped `userId` + a JSON
`tags` array column), the first OPEN / arbitrary-JSON object column
(`parameters`, JsonSchema.default({})), two boolean columns (`isDefault`,
`isDangerousCompatible` -> INTEGER 0/1), and two nullable string columns
(`apiKeyId`, `baseUrl`).
OPEN-JSON CONSTRAINT (tracked deferred seam): every `parameters` value is
either {} or a SINGLE-KEY object, so v4's JSON.stringify (insertion order)
and Rust's serde_json::Value (sorted keys) serialize byte-identically. A
multi-key object would diverge — see the module header in image_profiles.rs.
The fractional value 7.5 exercises js_number_to_json's float pass-through
inside the JSON text.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the
folders / tags / text_replacement_rules fixtures use. It is NOT the real
Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

ops.comment: New profile with a single-key fractional parameters object (exercises js_number_to_json float pass-through inside the JSON text), apiKeyId/baseUrl set, one tag, isDangerousCompatible true.
New profile with empty {} parameters, apiKeyId/baseUrl both null, empty tags, isDefault false.
Update seed row 1: rename, replace single-key parameters object, flip isDefault true->false. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Update seed row 2: replace the tags array (empty -> two ids). updatedAt pinned.
Delete the second created profile (New Local Comfy).
